name: Inter
classification_notes: Tall x-height geometric neo-grotesque designed for screen readability. Close relatives: Roboto, San Francisco, Akkurat.
summary: Open-source sans-serif designed specifically for screen readability, now one of the most widely used UI typefaces on the web.
description: Inter was built from the ground up for screen use, with a tall x-height, ink traps at small sizes, and optical size variants for text and display settings. It ships as a variable font covering nine weights with true italics, and includes over 30 OpenType features, 2000+ glyphs, and support for 147 languages across Latin, Greek and Cyrillic scripts. Used by Figma, Mozilla, GitLab, NASA and many others.
foundry_blurb: Inter is a free and open-source typeface family designed for computer screens, featuring a tall x-height for legibility and a comprehensive set of OpenType features including contextual alternates, stylistic sets and slashed zero.

name: Hovden Stitch
classification_notes: Decorative display typeface imitating embroidery or stitching patterns. Created as a personal experiment in 2002.
summary: An early experimental display typeface by Rasmus Andersson that mimics a classic stitching or embroidery aesthetic.
description: Hovden Stitch is a decorative typeface created in 2002 with letterforms that evoke hand-stitched or embroidered text. It is a personal early project by Andersson rather than a polished commercial release. Available from his projects archive.
foundry_blurb: A typeface from 2002 with a classic stitching and embroidery look.
